items.title: Starlink and Amazon Could Gain Access to Europe’s Satellite Airwaves
items.summary: The European Union plans to reserve most future satellite spectrum for regional firms while still allowing Starlink and Amazon to compete, reflecting Brussels' attempt to balance technological sovereignty with market openness in a strategically sensitive communications sector.

items.title: RF Digital Twins: Why 5G-Advanced and 6G Need Predictive Simulation
items.summary: RF Digital Twins: Why 5G-Advanced and 6G Need Predictive Simulation As wireless systems become more tightly coupled across…

items.title: 6G-Enabled Autonomous Systems Take Center Stage at NetRobiCS 2026
items.summary: The 19th International Workshop on Networked Robotics and Communication Systems (NetRobiCS 2026) successfully concluded yesterday in Tokyo, Japan, with the one6G Association serving as a key technical sponsor. Held as part of the IEEE INFOCOM 2026 conference, […] The post 6G-Enabled Autonomous Systems Take Center...

items.title: IEEE Society Helps Researchers Meet Their Next Corporate Backer
items.summary: The IEEE Communications Society (ComSoc) ’s Research Collaboration Pitch Session initiative is proving to be a catalyst for meaningful engagement between academic researchers and industry innovators. Launched last year, the program connects promising researchers with industry leaders who can offer them funding,...

items.title: Why RF Coexistence Testing Is Critical for Shared Spectrum
items.summary: A comprehensive review of how spectrum congestion, dynamic sharing, and cognitive radio systems are reshaping RF coexistence testing for military and commercial applications. What Attendees will Learn Why spectrum congestion threatens wireless reliability — Explore how over 30 billion connected devices, more than...

items.title: Ericsson predicts more FWA, uplink traffic as network demand transforms
items.summary: Ericsson sees FWA, satellite and AI-driven video uploads as big drivers of mobile network traffic growth.

items.title: 5G core reaches a turning point on power and scale
items.summary: As operators add capacity to support growing workloads, power efficiency and long-term flexibility are becoming core considerations. That shift is shaping how 5G core infrastructure is planned and deployed.

items.title: Newmont advances mining automation with Ericsson Enterprise Wireless Solutions in Australia
items.summary: Learn how Ericsson is helping Newmont bring private 5G to life—powering everything from giant autonomous drills to the smart tools miners could soon use every day.

items.title: ExteNet seeks buyer, breathing room amid small cell challenges
items.summary: ExteNet seeks a buyer for its outdoor small cell and DAS unit as a looming cash crunch and shifting carrier demands squeeze the business.

items.title: Blue Planet, Telefónica Deutschland create 5G network slices with AI
items.summary: According to Kailem Anderson with Blue Planet, the design of 5G network slices has taken surprisingly long – sometimes weeks.

items.title: AWS-3 auction concludes, topping $3.5B
items.summary: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) concluded its first spectrum auction in four years, raising more than $3.5 billion through the sale of 200 AWS-3 licenses.

items.title: Are telcos doing network APIs all wrong?
items.summary: Network APIs were supposed to unlock 5G’s next revenue wave. Years later, telcos are still wrestling with the basics. Are they barking up the wrong tree?

items.title: Enterprises are losing control of AI stacks but telcos can help, says IBM
items.summary: According to a new IBM study, “only 9% of executives say they have an excellent understanding of their dependencies on AI vendors, models and infrastructure."

items.title: Carolina West Wireless is shutting down in deal with Verizon
items.summary: Carolina West Wireless struck an agreement to transition its wireless network to Verizon and will end its wireless services effective September 30, 2026. Terms were not disclosed.

items.title: Verizon: 'We are pushing hard toward Level 4 autonomy in critical segments'
items.summary: Verizon CTO Yago Tenorio says the carrier is nearing Level 3 network autonomy and pushing toward Level 4, with Claude Code rolled out to its full 33,000-person technology team.

items.title: Opinion: Before networks can run themselves, engineers must teach AI what the network means
items.summary: In the telecom projects I’m working on now, the most important network conversation has not started with autonomous networks.

items.title: Opinion: The biggest news at DTW was a protocol most people missed
items.summary: Huawei's A2A-T protocol could become the coordination layer for multi-vendor AI agents in autonomous telecom networks.
